message:
feat(render): add row_cells_mut_span() for bulk mutable row access with dirty tracking

Add Buffer::row_cells_mut_span(y, x0, x1) that returns a mutable slice
of cells for the half-open column range [x0, x1) on row y. The range is
clamped to buffer width, the span is marked dirty once via
mark_dirty_span(), and None is returned for out-of-bounds rows or
reversed/empty ranges.

This is a performance-oriented primitive for callers that need to mutate
contiguous runs of cells (e.g. set_style_area, fill, block title clear)
without paying per-cell get_mut() overhead and its per-cell dirty mark.
Callers are responsible for scissor/opacity clipping before calling.

Tests:
- row_cells_mut_span_marks_once_and_returns_slice: verifies slice
  length, content writes, dirty row flag, and exact dirty span
- row_cells_mut_span_clamps_to_buffer_width: verifies clamping of
  x1=99 to buffer width=5, correct slice length, and dirty span
- row_cells_mut_span_rejects_reversed_ranges: verifies None return
  and no dirty marking for x0 > x1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
